allOf.description: Production cannot bypass authentication.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve must not enable LEAPVIEW_DEV_AUTH_BYPASS

allOf.description: OIDC settings are all-or-none in production.
allOf.x-error-message: production OIDC auth requires LEAPVIEW_OIDC_ISSUER_URL, LEAPVIEW_OIDC_CLIENT_ID, LEAPVIEW_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET, and LEAPVIEW_OIDC_CALLBACK_URL

allOf.description: Azure settings are all-or-none in production.
allOf.x-error-message: production Azure auth requires LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CLIENT_ID, LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET, and LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CALLBACK_URL

allOf.description: Production requires local, API-token-only, OIDC, or Azure authentication.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires OIDC auth env vars, Azure auth env vars, LEAPVIEW_LOCAL_AUTH, or LEAPVIEW_API_TOKEN_ONLY_AUTH

allOf.description: Production requires a CSRF key with at least 32 characters.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_CSRF_KEY with at least 32 characters

allOf.description: Production requires a metrics bearer token with at least 32 characters.
allOf.x-error-message: production metrics scraping requires LEAPVIEW_METRICS_BEARER_TOKEN with at least 32 characters

allOf.description: Production requires a canonical public URL.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_PUBLIC_URL

allOf.description: The production public URL must be an HTTPS origin without a path, query, fragment, or credentials.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_PUBLIC_URL to be an https origin

allOf.description: An external production MCP OAuth issuer must use HTTPS.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_MCP_OAUTH_ISSUER_URL to be an https URL

allOf.description: Production derives an allowed host from its public URL, explicit hosts, or a browser-auth callback host.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_PUBLIC_URL, LEAPVIEW_ALLOWED_HOSTS, or an OIDC/Azure callback URL host

allOf.description: Production browser authentication requires secure cookies unless API-token-only mode is also enabled.
allOf.x-error-message: production browser auth requires LEAPVIEW_COOKIE_SECURE=true

allOf.description: The production OIDC issuer must use HTTPS.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be an https URL

allOf.description: The production OIDC callback must use HTTPS.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_OIDC_CALLBACK_URL to be an https URL

allOf.description: The OIDC provider identifier must be route-safe.
allOf.x-error-message: LEAPVIEW_OIDC_PROVIDER_ID must be a route-safe slug containing only letters, numbers, dots, underscores, or dashes

allOf.description: The production Azure callback must use HTTPS.
allOf.x-error-message: production serve requires LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CALLBACK_URL to be an https URL

allOf.description: A configured production SCIM token must contain at least 32 characters.
allOf.x-error-message: production SCIM provisioning requires LEAPVIEW_SCIM_BEARER_TOKEN with at least 32 characters

allOf.description: Managed data uses a supported storage backend.
allOf.x-error-message: LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_BACKEND must be local or s3

allOf.description: Every managed-data backend requires a private local runtime and staging directory.
allOf.x-error-message: managed-data storage requires LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_DIR

allOf.description: The S3 managed-data backend requires a bucket and region.
allOf.x-error-message: S3 managed-data storage requires LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_BUCKET and LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_REGION

allOf.description: Managed-data S3 credentials are either omitted or configured as a complete key pair.
allOf.x-error-message: managed-data S3 credentials require both LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID and LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY; a session token also requires that pair

allOf.description: Managed-data upload, session, garbage-collection, and free-space limits are positive.
allOf.x-error-message: managed-data limits, durations, and free-space thresholds must be positive

allOf.description: The managed-data revision limit is at least the per-file limit.
allOf.x-error-message: LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MAX_REVISION_BYTES must be at least LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MAX_FILE_BYTES

LEAPVIEW_AGENT_API_KEY.description: API key for the configured agent model provider.

LEAPVIEW_ALLOWED_HOSTS.description: Comma- or whitespace-separated exact hosts and wildcard suffixes accepted in production.

LEAPVIEW_API_TOKEN.description: API token used by non-interactive CLI commands.

LEAPVIEW_API_TOKEN_ONLY_AUTH.description: Disable browser authentication and accept API tokens only.

LEAPVIEW_ASSET_VERSION.description: Optional browser asset cache-busting version override.

LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CALLBACK_URL.description: HTTPS callback URL registered with Azure AD or Entra ID.

LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CLIENT_ID.description: Azure AD or Entra ID OAuth client identifier.

LEAPVIEW_AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET.description: Azure AD or Entra ID OAuth client secret.

LEAPVIEW_AZURE_TENANT.description: Optional Azure AD or Entra ID tenant identifier.

LEAPVIEW_BASE_URL.description: Base URL used by browser QA tooling.

LEAPVIEW_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL.description: Email assigned to the initial production administrator.

LEAPVIEW_BOOTSTRAP_CACHE_DIR.description: Download cache directory used by dataset bootstrap tools.

LEAPVIEW_BOOTSTRAP_FORCE.description: Force dataset bootstrap tools to refresh existing files.

LEAPVIEW_CLI_CONFIG.description: Path to the local CLI target and token configuration file.

LEAPVIEW_COOKIE_SECURE.description: Secure-cookie override; defaults to true for production browser authentication.

LEAPVIEW_CSRF_KEY.description: Key used for CSRF protection and OAuth state cookies; production requires at least 32 characters.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_API_TOKEN.description: Static bearer credential accepted by the public API in development; replace the development default on shared machines.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_AUTH_BYPASS.description: Bypass authentication in development; forbidden in production.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_LOG_LINES.description: Number of log lines shown by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_PORT_COUNT.description: Number of ports scanned by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_PORT_START.description: First port scanned by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_PROJECT.description: Project published by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_READY_ATTEMPTS.description: Readiness attempts made by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_READY_INTERVAL.description: Delay between managed development server readiness attempts.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_RESTART.description: Force the managed development server to restart.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_SKIP_PUBLISH.description: Skip automatic project publishing in the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DEV_WORKTREE.description: Worktree path exported by the managed development server.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKDB_DIR.description: Directory containing node-local DuckDB runtime state.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKDB_NODE_MAX_THREADS.description: Execution-thread limit shared by all work in the process-owned DuckDB instance.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKDB_NODE_MEMORY_MAX_BYTES.description: Memory limit for the process-owned DuckDB instance.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKDB_NODE_TEMP_MAX_BYTES.description: Temporary-storage limit for the process-owned DuckDB instance.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKDB_TEMP_DIR.description: Private temporary-storage directory for the process-owned DuckDB instance; defaults beneath LEAPVIEW_HOME.

LEAPVIEW_DUCKLAKE_CATALOG_PATH.description: Path to the node's DuckDB-backed DuckLake catalog.

LEAPVIEW_ENVIRONMENT.description: Single serving environment permanently bound to this LeapView instance.

LEAPVIEW_HEALTHCHECK_URL.description: Explicit readiness URL used by the healthcheck command.

LEAPVIEW_HOME.description: Instance state directory containing databases, artifacts, and runtime files.

LEAPVIEW_IMAGE.description: Immutable LeapView OCI image reference consumed by deployment tooling.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_BACKEND.description: Storage backend for project-global managed data; supported values are local and s3.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_DIR.description: Private local root for managed-data objects, upload staging, and verified runtime views; defaults beneath LEAPVIEW_HOME.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_GC_GRACE_PERIOD.description: Minimum age of unreferenced managed-data objects before garbage collection.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MAX_FILES.description: Maximum number of files in one managed-data revision.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MAX_FILE_BYTES.description: Maximum size in bytes of one managed-data file.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MAX_REVISION_BYTES.description: Maximum total size in bytes of one managed-data revision.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_MIN_FREE_BYTES.description: Minimum free bytes required before accepting local managed-data uploads.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID.description: Optional S3 access-key identifier for managed-data storage.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_BUCKET.description: S3 bucket used for managed-data objects and staging.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_PATH_STYLE.description: Use path-style addressing for S3-compatible managed-data storage.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_PREFIX.description: Object-key prefix for managed data in the configured S3 bucket.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_REGION.description: S3 region used for managed-data requests.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_K.description: Optional S3 secret access key for managed-data storage.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_S3_SESSION_TOKEN.description: Optional temporary S3 session token for managed-data storage.

LEAPVIEW_MANAGED_DATA_UPLOAD_SESSION_TTL.description: Lifetime of an incomplete managed-data upload session.

LEAPVIEW_MAP_ASSET_DIR.description: Local root containing the verified, content-addressed basemap package.

LEAPVIEW_MCP_OAUTH_ISSUER_URL.description: Optional external OAuth issuer for MCP JWT access tokens; when omitted, LeapView provides the MCP authorization server.

LEAPVIEW_METRICS_BEARER_TOKEN.description: Bearer token protecting the Prometheus metrics endpoint; production requires at least 32 characters.

LEAPVIEW_OIDC_CALLBACK_URL.description: HTTPS callback URL registered with the generic OIDC provider.

LEAPVIEW_OIDC_ISSUER_URL.description: HTTPS issuer URL for the generic OIDC provider.

LEAPVIEW_OIDC_PROVIDER_ID.description: Route-safe identifier for the generic OIDC provider.

LEAPVIEW_OIDC_SCOPES.description: Comma- or whitespace-separated additional OIDC scopes.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_ENFORCE_THRESHOLDS.description: Fail dashboard performance QA when phase latency or query-count thresholds are exceeded.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_ITERATIONS.description: Measured interaction iterations run by the configured dashboard performance scenario.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_LOG.description: Development server log consumed by dashboard performance QA.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_MAX_ALL_TARGET_P95_MS.description: Maximum all-target settlement p95 when performance thresholds are enforced.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_MAX_CRITICAL_KPI_P95_MS.description: Maximum critical-KPI settlement p95 when performance thresholds are enforced.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_MAX_FIRST_TARGET_PAINT_P95.description: Maximum first-target paint p95 when performance thresholds are enforced.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_MAX_OPTIMISTIC_FEEDBACK_P9.description: Maximum local optimistic-feedback p95 when performance thresholds are enforced.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_MAX_QUERIES.description: Maximum physical queries per measured refresh when performance thresholds are enforced.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_OUTPUT.description: Optional JSON output path; defaults to a suite-specific file under .tmp.

LEAPVIEW_PERF_SCENARIO.description: Path to a dashboard performance scenario manifest.

LEAPVIEW_PRODUCTION.description: Enable production serving and validation behavior.

LEAPVIEW_PUBLIC_URL.description: Canonical externally visible LeapView origin used for MCP resource identity and OAuth discovery.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_NODE_MAX_BYTES.description: Maximum conservatively retained Arrow cache bytes for the node.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_NODE_MAX_ENTRIES.description: Maximum cached result entries retained by the node.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_RUNTIME_MAX_BYTES.description: Maximum conservatively retained Arrow cache bytes for one serving generation.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_RUNTIME_MAX_ENTRIES.description: Maximum cached result entries retained by one serving generation.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_WORKSPACE_MAX_BYTES.description: Maximum conservatively retained Arrow cache bytes across one workspace's serving generations.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_CACHE_WORKSPACE_MAX_ENTRI.description: Maximum cached result entries retained across one workspace's serving generations.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_RESULT_MAX_BYTES.description: Maximum conservatively retained Arrow bytes for one logical analytical operation.

LEAPVIEW_QUERY_RESULT_MAX_ROWS.description: Maximum rows retained by one logical analytical operation.

LEAPVIEW_REFRESH_JOB_LEASE_TIMEOUT.description: Lease duration before an abandoned refresh job may be reclaimed.

LEAPVIEW_SCIM_BEARER_TOKEN.description: Bearer token enabling SCIM provisioning; production requires at least 32 characters when set.

LEAPVIEW_SITE_BASE_URL.description: Externally visible HTTP(S) origin used for canonical URLs, discovery documents, and transport policy.

LEAPVIEW_SITE_SHOWCASE_EMBED_URL.description: Optional public dashboard embed URL that enables the live /showcase route and its exact frame-src policy.

LEAPVIEW_SMOKE_PORT.description: Host port used by the production image smoke test.

LEAPVIEW_TOKEN_HASH_KEY.description: Optional dedicated key for deterministic API-token fingerprints; falls back to the CSRF key.

LEAPVIEW_TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS.description: Trust client-address headers only when a trusted proxy overwrites them.

LEAPVIEW_WAREHOUSE_DSN.description: Example externally supplied warehouse connection credential.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_BACKGROUND_EXECUTION_T.description: Maximum execution time for background work.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_BACKGROUND_MAX_QUEUED_.description: Maximum queued background operations for one workspace.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_BACKGROUND_QUEUE_TIMEO.description: Maximum time background work may wait for admission.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_BACKGROUND_RESERVED_RU.description: Capacity reserved for background work when background demand is queued.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_CONTROL_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Maximum execution time for control work.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_CONTROL_QUEUE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time control work may wait for admission.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_CONTROL_RESERVED_RUNNI.description: Capacity reserved for node-scoped control work when control demand is queued.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_INTERACTIVE_EXECUTION_.description: Maximum execution time for interactive work.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_INTERACTIVE_MAX_QUEUED.description: Maximum queued interactive operations for one workspace.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_INTERACTIVE_QUEUE_TIME.description: Maximum time interactive work may wait for admission.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_INTERACTIVE_RESERVED_R.description: Capacity reserved for interactive work when interactive demand is queued.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAINTENANCE_EXECUTION_.description: Maximum execution time for maintenance work.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAINTENANCE_MAX_QUEUED.description: Maximum queued maintenance operations; zero makes maintenance skip when saturated.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAINTENANCE_QUEUE_TIME.description: Maximum time maintenance may wait; zero means no wait.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAINTENANCE_RESERVED_R.description: Capacity reserved for maintenance work when maintenance demand is queued.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAX_QUEUED.description: Maximum queued operations across all workload classes.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_MAX_RUNNING.description: Maximum operations running concurrently on this node.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_REFRESH_EXECUTION_TIME.description: Maximum execution time for refresh work; zero leaves the deadline to the workflow.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_REFRESH_MAX_QUEUED_PER.description: Maximum queued refresh operations for one workspace.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_REFRESH_QUEUE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time refresh work may wait for admission.

LEAPVIEW_WORKLOAD_REFRESH_RESERVED_RUNNI.description: Capacity reserved for refresh work when refresh demand is queued.
